## Task: Move FileServiceOptions from root namespace to TestProject.Configuration
## Step: review

`FileServiceOptions` is the only type in the root `TestProject` namespace, inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which organizes types into `TestProject.Models`, `TestProject.Services`, `TestProject.Controllers`. Move it to `TestProject.Configuration`: create a new file `Configuration/FileServiceOptions.cs` (relative to the repo root, alongside the existing top-level folders) containing the unchanged class body but with `namespace TestProject.Configuration;`. Delete the old `FileServiceOptions.cs` at the repo root. Update `Program.cs` to add `using TestProject.Configuration;` (or use the fully-qualified name `TestProject.Configuration.FileServiceOptions` in the `Configure<...>(...)` call). Update `Services/FileService.cs` to add `using TestProject.Configuration;`. The FileServiceOptionsTests.cs file was deleted by task-4, so no test-side namespace update is needed. Verification: `dotnet build TestProject.sln` succeeds; `dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj` passes.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: CORE RELOCATION IS CORRECT AND VERIFIED. The four in-scope changes all landed exactly as specified: (1) new Configuration/FileServiceOptions.cs declares `namespace TestProject.Configuration;` with the unchanged class body; (2) the old root FileServiceOptions.cs is deleted (git status: `D FileServiceOptions.cs`); (3) Program.cs now uses the fully-qualified `TestProject.Configuration.FileServiceOptions` in the Configure<T>(...) call (one of the two options the task permitted); (4) Services/FileService.cs gained `using TestProject.Configuration;`. A fifth necessary edit — adding `using TestProject.Configuration;` to TestProject.Tests/Services/FileServiceTests.cs — is justified: that file directly constructs `new FileServiceOptions` (lines 56, 117, 852), so without it the test project would not compile. Verification confirmed: `dotnet build TestProject.sln` => 0 warnings / 0 errors; `dotnet test TestProject.Tests/TestProject.Tests.csproj` => Passed: 246, Failed: 0. No behavior change was introduced by the move (class body is byte-identical aside from the namespace), satisfying the refactor-preservation requirement.

The blocker is SCOPE EXPANSION. The task description explicitly states: 'The FileServiceOptionsTests.cs file was deleted by task-4, so no test-side namespace update is needed.' Despite this, the implementer created two brand-new, untracked test files (~350 lines combined): TestProject.Tests/FileServiceOptionsTests.cs (reflection-based identity/contract tests) and TestProject.Tests/FileServiceOptionsNamespaceTests.cs (file-inspection tests for the relocation mechanics). Neither file existed in HEAD and neither was requested. The task was a mechanical move + reference updates; adding new tests is out of scope and contradicts the explicit 'no test-side work needed' note. These should be removed (or deferred to a dedicated test-authoring task) so this change stays a pure refactor. This is the only substantive issue — the relocation mechanics themselves are flawless.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }
